level_signals: Base pay range $173K–$255K depending on location, consistent with Senior PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years in Product Management; Ideally with previous background owning growth/distribution channels
Track record shipping consumer financial or regulated products; comfort partnering with Risk/Credit, Legal, and Compliance.
Deep fluency in experiment design, incrementality/attribution, and CAC→LTV optimization.
Strong UX instincts for moment‑of‑intent surfaces; ability to work across web, app, and partner integrations.
AI tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and others to research, analyze, visualize experiences and help develop better product vision
Data chops (SQL/Amplitude‑level self‑serve) and a bias for shipping, learning, and iterating quickly.
Excellent cross‑functional leadership; experience leading embedded eng/design pods.
Bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years in Product Management; Ideally with previous background owning growth/distribution channels

scope.surface: DTC shopping and offers experience across Affirm App and Web (checkout flows, consumer shopping preferences, offers, acquisition programs)
core_responsibilities: Define and own the Shopping Experiences roadmap for new growth channels, setting clear hypotheses, success metrics, and sequencing

Launch and scale checkout flows, offers in app and web, consumer shopping preferences across app and web, partnering closely with Engineering and Design to deliver seamless, high-conversion onboarding experiences.
Design and execute acquisition programs (e.g., referrals, exploratory partner pilots) with measurable CAC→LTV impact.
Own funnel performance end-to-end: track weekly acquisitions, 30-day activation, drop-offs by step, and implement robust incrementality testing.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Risk, Compliance, Marketing, and the Card Foundations team to align dependencies and ensure safe, scalable growth.
Continuously experiment and iterate to unlock new conversion levers, maximize engagement, and expand card and consumer usage

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade 9; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan at a discount); equity rewards mentioned as part of total compensation

green_flags: Compensation ranges disclosed and competitive ($173K–$255K base depending on location)
Remote-first company; role eligible for most US states
Clear product surface with well-defined ownership (DTC shopping, offers, checkout)
Specific metrics called out (CAC→LTV, 30-day activation, weekly acquisitions) — signals data-driven culture
100% subsidized medical coverage for employee and dependents is a strong benefit
ESPP and equity rewards mentioned in addition to base
red_flags: AI tools requirement (Claude Code, Cursor) listed among unconditional 'What we look for' bullets — unusual and ambiguous whether this is truly hard or aspirational
Location pay differential is significant ($20K+ gap between high-cost states and others)
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-US-citizen applicants
No explicit mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior PM' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description: BNPL/consumer fintech)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not an explicit JD field)
compensation.base_range_usd set to [173000, 255000] as the full national range spanning both geo tiers
ambiguities: AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor) appear in an unconditional 'What we look for' bullet — could be hard requirement or aspirational; treated as hard here per rule but flagged as ambiguous
Growth/distribution channel background is labeled 'Ideally' in the same bullet as the hard 4+ years requirement — extracted as a soft nice-to-have separate from the hard years requirement
JD lists '#LI-Remote' tag and states remote-first, but does not explicitly confirm visa sponsorship; work_auth left as 'open' per absence of restriction language
